requirements.description: Research must focus on next-generation AI agents defined as autonomous systems powered by large AI language models (LLMs) or large multimodal models, capable of interpreting instructions, generating plans, using tools, and performing actions autonomously to achieve specified goals based on high-level instructions.
Proposals must address robust planning, reasoning, and search mechanisms that allow agents to approach complex tasks by breaking them down into structured subgoals.
Proposals must address effective memory and state management, including balanced integration of short-term and external long-term memory solutions to maintain coherent long-term agent interactions.
Integration of external tools and APIs is an essential required element to overcome limitations of large AI models and enhance agent performance in tasks requiring accuracy and reliability.

Proposals must focus on at least one of the following research areas: (a) enhancing AI agent autonomy through advanced self-planning and self-optimization; (b) innovation in memory-augmented AI agents for robust long-term reasoning and lifelong learning; (c) developing advanced multi-agent frameworks tailored for collaborative agents, including mixed AI architectures; (d) advancing multimodal reasoning capabilities for real-world applications.
All proposals are expected to incorporate mechanisms for assessing and demonstrating progress, including qualitative and quantitative KPIs, benchmarking, and progress monitoring.
Proposals must demonstrate application in at least one Apply AI sector. The three specifically identified sectors are: healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and in-vehicle autonomous driving.
Effective AI agents must incorporate appropriate validation and monitoring techniques for agent behaviour as a required design element.
All proposals must allocate tasks for cohesion activities with the European Partnership on AI, Data and Robotics (ADRA) and with the CSA HORIZON-CL4-2025-03-HUMAN-18: GenAI4EU central Hub.
Communicable results must be shared with the European R&D community through the AI-on-demand platform.
Projects must link to the resources offered by the AI Factories, including the Data Labs.
When possible, proposals should build on and reuse public results from relevant previously funded actions.
